== Notes for [[Item:Q82807|Q82807]] ==

Original name '''Wa'il bin Umayyah'''

Abu al-As ibn Umayya:
His sons were the following:
Affan, father of Caliph Uthman.
Al-Hakam, the father of the Umayyad caliph Marwan I.
Al-Mughira, maternal great-grandfather of the Umayyad caliph Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan.
He also had daughters:
Safiyya, who married Abu Sufyan.
Their daughter Ramla was a wife of Muhammad.
Arwa, who married Amr ibn Hisham. Their daughter Asma was the second wife of Caliph Uthman.



Source: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abu_al-%27As_ibn_Umayyah

Founder of the Umayyad clans.

Wa'il ibn Umayyah
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Wa'il ibn Umayya, also known as Abu al-As [citation needed] was the son of Umayya ibn Abd Shams.

As his sons are mentioned:

* A'as ibn Wa'il, father of Amr ibn al-A'as[citation needed]
* Affan ibn Wa'il, father of Uthman[citation needed]
* Abu al-Hakim ibn Wa'il[citation needed]

Another source also mentions:

* Hakam ibn Wa'il, also known as "al-Hakam ibn Abi al-`As" [1] aka "Hakam Ibn Abi al-As Ibn Umayya" [2][citation needed]

He also had a daughter:

* Saffya binte abi al-A'as, who bore a daughter to Abu Safyan.

[[Category:Wikipedia overwrite]]
[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
